devanagari: सूत उवाच । नमो धर्माय महते नमः कृष्णाय वेधसे । ब्रह्मणेभ्यो नमस्कृत्य धर्मान् वक्ष्ये सनातनान् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca namo dharmāya mahate namaḥ kṛṣṇāya vedhase brahmaṇebhyo namaskṛtya dharmān vakṣye sanātanān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[locative single from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vakṣ ]

[nominative dual from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ vakṣ ], [vocative dual from √ vakṣ ], [accusative dual from √ vakṣ ], [locative single from √ vakṣ ]

[nominative dual from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vakṣ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ vakṣ ], [vocative single from √ vakṣ ], [vocative dual from √ vakṣ ], [accusative dual from √ vakṣ ]

translation:
Sūta said:
Salutation to the great virtue in the form of devotion to Lord Hari. Hail to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the creator of the universe. Having bowed down to Brāhmaṇas (who are devoted to Goḍ). I now enumerate the eternal courses of righteousness.

devanagari: एतद् वः कथितं विप्रा विष्णोश्चरितमद्भुतम् । भवद्भिः यदहं पृष्टो नराणां पुरुषोचितम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: etad vaḥ kathitaṃ viprā viṣṇoścaritamadbhutam bhavadbhiḥ yadahaṃ pṛṣṭo narāṇāṃ puruṣocitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kath class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ kath class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ uc class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ uc class 4 verb]

translation: Here has been narrated to you the miraculous story (in the form of this Bhāgavata) of Lord Viṣṇu, about which you asked me and which is really to the good of human beings (and is fit to be related and listened to in the assembly of pious persons).

devanagari: अत्र सङ्कीर्तितः साक्षात् सर्वपापहरो हरिः । नारायणो हृषीकेशो भगवान् सात्वतां पतिः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: atra saṅkīrtitaḥ sākṣāt sarvapāpaharo hariḥ nārāyaṇo hṛṣīkeśo bhagavān sātvatāṃ patiḥ
translation: Here has been glorified Lord Hari who destroys the sins of all; who is Nārāyaṇa, the controller of sense-organs and is the protector of his devotees (or Sātvatas)

devanagari: अत्र ब्रह्म परं गुह्यं जगतः प्रभवाप्ययम् । ज्ञानं च तदुपाख्यानं प्रोक्तं विज्ञानसंयुतम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: atra brahma paraṃ guhyaṃ jagataḥ prabhavāpyayam jñānaṃ ca tadupākhyānaṃ proktaṃ vijñānasaṃyutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ guh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ guh class 1 verb]

translation: Here has been described the Supreme attributeless Brahman which is the source as well as the end of all creation; also the topic of spiritual knowledge and realisation as well as means of realising them.

devanagari: भक्तियोगः समाख्यातो वैराग्यं च तदाश्रयम् । पारीक्षितं उपाख्यानं नारदाख्यानमेव च ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktiyogaḥ samākhyāto vairāgyaṃ ca tadāśrayam pārīkṣitaṃ upākhyānaṃ nāradākhyānameva ca
translation: Here has been delineated in details the Yoga or discipline of Devotion (which is both a means and an end in itself,) as well as renunciation (Vairāgya) which is brought about and thus depends on devotion. Then follows the story of the birth, etc. of king Parīkṣit which has been preceded by the story of the previous births of Nārada.

devanagari: प्रायोपवेशो राजर्षेः विप्रशापात् परीक्षितः । शुकस्य ब्रह्मर्षभस्य संवादश्च परीक्षितः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: prāyopaveśo rājarṣeḥ vipraśāpāt parīkṣitaḥ śukasya brahmarṣabhasya saṃvādaśca parīkṣitaḥ
translation: The vow of the royal sage Parīkṣit to fast unto death due to the curse imprecated on him by a Brāhmaṇa, the dialogue between king Parīkṣit and the prominent Brāhmaṇa Śuka (Here ends the first Skandha).

devanagari: योगधारणयोत्क्रान्तिः संवादो नारदाजयोः । अवतारानुगीतं च सर्गः प्राधानिकोऽग्रतः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yogadhāraṇayotkrāntiḥ saṃvādo nāradājayoḥ avatārānugītaṃ ca sargaḥ prādhāniko

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The ascent of the soul to higher regions when departed from the body through Yogic process; the dialogue between Nārada and god Brahmā; the description of the incarnation of god Viṣṇu in a serial order; the cosmic evolution from Prakṛtī from the beginning.

devanagari: विदुरोद्धवसंवादः क्षत्तृमैत्रेययोस्ततः । पुराणसंहिताप्रश्नो महापुरुषसंस्थितिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: viduroddhavasaṃvādaḥ kṣattṛmaitreyayostataḥ purāṇasaṃhitāpraśno mahāpuruṣasaṃsthitiḥ
translation: The conversation between Vidura and Uddhava and that between Vidura and Maitreya; an enquiry regarding the compilation of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa and the dormant state of the Supreme Man during Pralaya.

devanagari: ततः प्राकृतिकः सर्गः सप्त वैकृतिकाश्च ये । ततो ब्रह्माण्डसम्भूतिः वैराजः पुरुषो यतः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ prākṛtikaḥ sargaḥ sapta vaikṛtikāśca ye tato brahmāṇḍasambhūtiḥ vairājaḥ puruṣo yataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Then follows the description of the evolution of the universe from Prakṛti by the disturbance of its guṇas, the evolution of the seven categories which are both the cause and the effect as well as their modifications; then follows the formation of the egg of the universe from which Virāṭ Puruṣa was evolved.

devanagari: कालस्य स्थूलसूक्ष्मस्य गतिः पद्मसमुद्भवः । भुव उद्धरणेऽम्भोधेः हिरण्याक्षवधो यथा ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: kālasya sthūlasūkṣmasya gatiḥ padmasamudbhavaḥ bhuva uddharaṇe
translation: The nature of Time both gross and subtle; the blooming of the cosmic lotus; (containing 14 worlds—bhuvanas) lifting up of the earth from the bottom of the ocean and slaying the demon Hiraṇyākṣa.

devanagari: ऊर्ध्वतिर्यगवाक्सर्गो रुद्रसर्गस्तथैव च । अर्धनारीश्वरस्याथ यतः स्वायंभुवो मनुः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ūrdhvatiryagavāksargo rudrasargastathaiva ca ardhanārīśvarasyātha yataḥ svāyaṃbhuvo manuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Creation of the celestial world; the subhuman beings and of the subterranean regions; creation of Rudras; the constitution of one half of his person as male and the other half as female by god Brahmā, from which was born Manu called Svāyambhuva and of Śatarūpā the ideal first woman; the progeny of the wives of Karḍama, the Lord of Creation.

devanagari: शतरूपा च या स्त्रीणां आद्या प्रकृतिरुत्तमा । सन्तानो धर्मपत्‍नीनां कर्दमस्य प्रजापतेः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śatarūpā ca yā strīṇāṃ ādyā prakṛtiruttamā santāno dharmapat‍nīnāṃ kardamasya prajāpateḥ
translation: Creation of the celestial world; the subhuman beings and of the subterranean regions; creation of Rudras; the constitution of one half of his person as male and the other half as female by god Brahmā, from which was born Manu called Svāyambhuva and of Śatarūpā the ideal first woman; the progeny of the wives of Karḍama, the Lord of Creation.

devanagari: अवतारो भगवतः कपिलस्य महात्मनः । देवहूत्याश्च संवादः कपिलेन च धीमता ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: avatāro bhagavataḥ kapilasya mahātmanaḥ devahūtyāśca saṃvādaḥ kapilena ca dhīmatā
translation: The descent of the glorious Lord as the great-souled Kapila; the dialogue between the intelligent Kapila and his mother Devahūti.

devanagari: नवब्रह्मसमुत्पत्तिः दक्षयज्ञविनाशनम् । ध्रुवस्य चरितं पश्चात् पृथोः प्राचीनबर्हिषः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: navabrahmasamutpattiḥ dakṣayajñavināśanam dhruvasya caritaṃ paścāt pṛthoḥ prācīnabarhiṣaḥ
translation: The creation of the nine Lords of creation (Prajāpatis); interruption in the sacrifice of Dakṣa; the account of kings Dhruva, Pṛthu and Prācīnabarhis.

devanagari: नारदस्य च संवादः ततः प्रैयव्रतं द्विजाः । नाभेस्ततोऽनुचरितं ऋषभस्य भरतस्य च ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nāradasya ca saṃvādaḥ tataḥ praiyavrataṃ dvijāḥ nābhestato
translation: The conversation with Nārada; the story of king Priyavrata; and then O Brāhmaṇas, the account of Kings Nābhi, Ṛṣabha and Bharata.

devanagari: द्वीपवर्षसमुद्राणां गिरिनद्युपवर्णनम् । ज्योतिश्चक्रस्य संस्थानं पातालनरकस्थितिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: dvīpavarṣasamudrāṇāṃ girinadyupavarṇanam jyotiścakrasya saṃsthānaṃ pātālanarakasthitiḥ
translation: Description of island-continents and their subsections called Varṣas and seas separating the continents; the mountain-systems and the river-systems in this continent described; the disposition of the stellar system (stars, planets, etc.) and the arrangements of the subterranean regions and of hills.

devanagari: दक्षजन्म प्रचेतोभ्यः तत्पुत्रीणां च सन्ततिः । यतो देवासुरनराः तिर्यङ्नगखगादयः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: dakṣajanma pracetobhyaḥ tatputrīṇāṃ ca santatiḥ yato devāsuranarāḥ tiryaṅnagakhagādayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The birth of Dakṣa from Pracetas; the progeny of his daughters, viz. gods, demons, human beings, sub-human beings (beasts, etc.) trees, birds, etc.

devanagari: त्वाष्ट्रस्य जन्मनिधनं पुत्रयोश्च दितेर्द्विजाः । दैत्येश्वरस्य चरितं प्रह्रादस्य महात्मनः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tvāṣṭrasya janmanidhanaṃ putrayośca diterdvijāḥ daityeśvarasya caritaṃ prahrādasya mahātmanaḥ
translation: The birth and death of Vṛtra, the son of Tvaṣṭṛ and that of the two sons of Diti, viz. Hiraṇyākṣa and Hiraṇyakaśipu and the story of the noble soul Prahlāda (Here end Skandhas—VI & VII).

devanagari: मन्वन्तरानुकथनं गजेन्द्रस्य विमोक्षणम् । मन्वन्तरावताराश्च विष्णोर्हयशिरादयः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: manvantarānukathanaṃ gajendrasya vimokṣaṇam manvantarāvatārāśca viṣṇorhayaśirādayaḥ
translation: A connected account of the fourteen Manvantaras; deliverance of the king of elephants (from a giant Crocodile); Various incarnations of Viṣṇu such as Hayagrīva and others.

devanagari: कौर्मं धान्वतरं मात्स्यं वामनं च जगत्पतेः । क्षीरोदमथनं तद्वद् अमृतार्थे दिवौकसाम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: kaurmaṃ dhānvataraṃ mātsyaṃ vāmanaṃ ca jagatpateḥ kṣīrodamathanaṃ tadvad amṛtārthe divaukasām
translation: Viṣṇu’s descent like the divine tortoise, the divine fish, Narasiṃha (Half man—half lion); the churning of the ocean of milk for obtaining nectar by gods and demons.

devanagari: देवासुरमहायुद्धं राजवंशानुकीर्तनम् । इक्ष्वाकुजन्म तद्वंशः सुद्युम्नस्य महात्मनः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: devāsuramahāyuddhaṃ rājavaṃśānukīrtanam ikṣvākujanma tadvaṃśaḥ sudyumnasya mahātmanaḥ
translation: The great war between gods and Asuras; a description of royal dynasties; the birth of Ikṣvāku and his race as also of the great-souled Sudyumna.

devanagari: इलोपाख्यानमत्रोक्तं तारोपाख्यानमेव च । सूर्यवंशानुकथनं शशादाद्या नृगादयः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: ilopākhyānamatroktaṃ tāropākhyānameva ca sūryavaṃśānukathanaṃ śaśādādyā nṛgādayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: Here have been described the stories of Ilā and of Tārā; the history of the solar race of kings such as Śaśāda, Nṛga and others.

devanagari: सौकन्यं चाथ शर्यातेः ककुत्स्थस्य च धीमतः । खट्वाङ्गस्य च मान्धातुः सौभरेः सगरस्य च ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: saukanyaṃ cātha śaryāteḥ kakutsthasya ca dhīmataḥ khaṭvāṅgasya ca māndhātuḥ saubhareḥ sagarasya ca
translation: The narrative of Sukanyā, the daughter of Śaryāti and of the highly intelligent Kākutstha and of Khaṭvāṅga, of Māndhātṛ, of sage Saubhari and of king Sagara.

devanagari: रामस्य कोशलेन्द्रस्य चरितं किल्बिषापहम् । निमेरङ्गपरित्यागो जनकानां च सम्भवः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: rāmasya kośalendrasya caritaṃ kilbiṣāpaham nimeraṅgaparityāgo janakānāṃ ca sambhavaḥ
translation: The story of Rāma, the king of Kosala which eradicates all sins; Nimi and his giving up of his body; the origin of the race of Janakas.

devanagari: रामस्य भार्गवेन्द्रस्य निःक्षतृईकरणं भुवः । ऐलस्य सोमवंशस्य ययातेर्नहुषस्य च ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: rāmasya bhārgavendrasya niḥkṣatṛīkaraṇaṃ bhuvaḥ ailasya somavaṃśasya yayāternahuṣasya ca
translation: The account of Paraśurāma, the chief of the Bhṛgu clan, and his wiping out the Kṣattriya race; the account of the lunar race which started from Purūravas, the son of Ilā and of Yayāti and Nahuṣa.

devanagari: दौष्मन्तेर्भरतस्यापि शान्तनोस्तत्सुतस्य च । ययातेर्ज्येष्ठपुत्रस्य यदोर्वंशोऽनुकीर्तितः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: dauṣmanterbharatasyāpi śāntanostatsutasya ca yayāterjyeṣṭhaputrasya yadorvaṃśo
translation: The accounts of Bharata, the sun of Duṣyanta, of Śantanu and his son. The race of Yadu, the eldest son of Yayāti has been described—the race in which the Lord of the universe under the name Kṛṣṇa took his incarnation; his birth in the house of Vasudeva and growing up in Gokula.

devanagari: यत्रावतीर्णो भगवान् कृष्णाख्यो जगदीश्वरः । वसुदेवगृहे जन्म ततो वृद्धिश्च गोकुले ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yatrāvatīrṇo bhagavān kṛṣṇākhyo jagadīśvaraḥ vasudevagṛhe janma tato vṛddhiśca gokule
translation: The accounts of Bharata, the sun of Duṣyanta, of Śantanu and his son. The race of Yadu, the eldest son of Yayāti has been described—the race in which the Lord of the universe under the name Kṛṣṇa took his incarnation; his birth in the house of Vasudeva and growing up in Gokula.

devanagari: तस्य कर्माण्यपाराणि कीर्तितान्यसुरद्विषः । पूतनासुपयःपानं शकटोच्चाटनं शिशोः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya karmāṇyapārāṇi kīrtitānyasuradviṣaḥ pūtanāsupayaḥpānaṃ śakaṭoccāṭanaṃ śiśoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kīrt class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kīrt class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ kīrt class 10 verb]

translation: The innumerable and miraculous deeds of the enemy of Asuras, viz. of Kṣṛṇa have been described: drinking up of the life of Pūtanā along with the milk in her breast, the kicking up of Śakaṭa while a child.

devanagari: तृणावर्तस्य निष्पेषः तथैव बकवत्सयोः । धेनुकस्य सहभ्रातुः प्रलम्बस्य च संक्षयः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛṇāvartasya niṣpeṣaḥ tathaiva bakavatsayoḥ dhenukasya sahabhrātuḥ pralambasya ca saṃkṣayaḥ
translation: Crushing of the demon Tṛṇāvarta as well as of Baka and Vatsa and of Dhenuka along with his brother and slaying of Pralamba.

devanagari: गोपानां च परित्राणं दावाग्नेः परिसर्पतः । दमनं कालियस्याहेः महाहेर्नन्दमोक्षणम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: gopānāṃ ca paritrāṇaṃ dāvāgneḥ parisarpataḥ damanaṃ kāliyasyāheḥ mahāhernandamokṣaṇam
translation: Protection of the cow-herds from the encircling forest conflagration; subjugation of the great serpent Kāliya and the deliverance of Nanda.

devanagari: व्रतचर्या तु कन्यानां यत्र तुष्टोऽच्युतो व्रतैः । प्रसादो यज्ञपत्‍नीभ्यो विप्राणां चानुतापनम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vratacaryā tu kanyānāṃ yatra tuṣṭo prasādo yajñapat‍nībhyo viprāṇāṃ cānutāpanam
translation: The observance of the Kātyāyanī vow by Gopa girls whereby Lord Kṛṣṇa was pleased (and granted a boon): Showering of grace on the wives of Brāhmaṇa sacrificers; the repentance of Brāhmaṇas for not recognising the real nature of the Lord.

devanagari: गोवर्धनोद्धारणं च शक्रस्य सुरभेरथ । यज्ञभिषेकं कृष्णस्य स्त्रीभिः क्रीडा च रात्रिषु ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: govardhanoddhāraṇaṃ ca śakrasya surabheratha yajñabhiṣekaṃ kṛṣṇasya strībhiḥ krīḍā ca rātriṣu
translation: Lifting up of the mount Govardhana; Indra and the heavenly cow Surabhi worship Kṛṣṇa and bathe him in milk; Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes (Rāsakrīḍā) with Gopīs during several nights.

devanagari: शङ्खचूडस्य दुर्बुद्धेः वधोऽरिष्टस्य केशिनः । अक्रूरागमनं पश्चात् प्रस्थानं रामकृष्णयोः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhacūḍasya durbuddheḥ vadho akrūrāgamanaṃ paścāt prasthānaṃ rāmakṛṣṇayoḥ
translation: Slaying of the evil-minded Śaṅkhacūḍa and of demons Ariṣṭa and Keśin; arrival of Akrūra; subsequent departure of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: व्रजस्त्रीणां विलापश्च मथुरालोकनं ततः । गजमुष्टिकचाणूर कंसादीनां तथा वधः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: vrajastrīṇāṃ vilāpaśca mathurālokanaṃ tataḥ gajamuṣṭikacāṇūra kaṃsādīnāṃ tathā vadhaḥ
translation: The wailing of Gopīs of Vraja; visit to different places in Mathurā; killing of the elephant (Kuvalayāpīḍa) and of Muṣṭika, Cāṇūra, Kaṃsa and others (his eight brothers).

devanagari: मृतस्यानयनं सूनोः पुनः सान्दीपनेर्गुरोः । मथुरायां निवसता यदुचक्रस्य यत्प्रियम् । कृतमुद्धवरामाभ्यां युतेन हरिणा द्विजाः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛtasyānayanaṃ sūnoḥ punaḥ sāndīpanerguroḥ mathurāyāṃ nivasatā yaducakrasya yatpriyam kṛtamuddhavarāmābhyāṃ yutena hariṇā dvijāḥ

[instrumental single from √ yu class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ yu class 6 verb], [instrumental single from √ yu class 9 verb]

translation: Restoring to his preceptor Sāndīpani his dead son; good offices to the Yādava clansmen at Mathurā.

devanagari: जरासन्धसमानीत सैन्यस्य बहुशो वधः । घातनं यवनेन्द्रस्य कुशस्थल्या निवेशनम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: jarāsandhasamānīta sainyasya bahuśo vadhaḥ ghātanaṃ yavanendrasya kuśasthalyā niveśanam
translation: Annihilation of the armies brought by Jarāsandha many times, with the help of Uddhava and Balarāma, O Brāhmaṇas; killing of the king of Yavanas through Mucakunda; founding of the city of Kuśasthalī or Dvārakā.

devanagari: आदानं पारिजातस्य सुधर्मायाः सुरालयात् । रुक्मिण्या हरणं युद्धे प्रमथ्य द्विषतो हरेः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: ādānaṃ pārijātasya sudharmāyāḥ surālayāt rukmiṇyā haraṇaṃ yuddhe pramathya dviṣato hareḥ

translation: Bringing of heavenly Pārijāta tree and the celestial assembly hall called Sudharmā; taking away of Rukmiṇī by Hari after destroying the enemies in a battle.

devanagari: हरस्य जृम्भणं युद्धे बाणस्य भुजकृन्तनम् । प्राग्ज्योतिषपतिं हत्वा कन्यानां हरणं च यत् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: harasya jṛmbhaṇaṃ yuddhe bāṇasya bhujakṛntanam prāgjyotiṣapatiṃ hatvā kanyānāṃ haraṇaṃ ca yat

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Yawning of god Śiva while fighting with Kṛṣṇa in defence of Bāṇa; chopping of Bāṇa’s arms in the battle-; killing of Naraka the king of Prāgjyotiṣa and taking away of the girls detained by him.

devanagari: चैद्यपौण्ड्रकशाल्वानां दन्तवक्रस्य दुर्मतेः । शम्बरो द्विविदः पीठो मुरः पञ्चजनादयः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: caidyapauṇḍrakaśālvānāṃ dantavakrasya durmateḥ śambaro dvividaḥ pīṭho muraḥ pañcajanādayaḥ
translation: The killing of Śiśupāla, Pauṇḍraka, Śālva and of the wicked-minded Dantavaktra; the destruction of the monkey Dvivida, Pīṭha, Mura and Pañcajanas and others.

devanagari: माहात्म्यं च वधस्तेषां वाराणस्याश्च दाहनम् । भारावतरणं भूमेः निमित्तीकृत्य पाण्डवान् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: māhātmyaṃ ca vadhasteṣāṃ vārāṇasyāśca dāhanam bhārāvataraṇaṃ bhūmeḥ nimittīkṛtya pāṇḍavān
translation:
The power of the above kings and demons and their slaying; burning of the city of Vārāṇasī; lightening of the burden of the earth by making the Pāṇḍavas as his instruments.
(Here ends the Tenth Skandha)

devanagari: विप्रशापापदेशेन संहारः स्वकुलस्य च । उद्धवस्य च संवादो वसुदेवस्य चाद्भुतः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: vipraśāpāpadeśena saṃhāraḥ svakulasya ca uddhavasya ca saṃvādo vasudevasya cādbhutaḥ
translation: The complete destruction of his own race under the pretext of Brāhmaṇa’s curse; the wonderful dialogue between. Uddhava and Vāsudeva.

devanagari: यत्रात्मविद्या ह्यखिला प्रोक्ता धर्मविनिर्णयः । ततो मर्त्यपरित्याग आत्मयोगानुभावतः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yatrātmavidyā hyakhilā proktā dharmavinirṇayaḥ tato martyaparityāga ātmayogānubhāvataḥ
translation:
In that dialogue, the entire range of the science of Āmnan as well as the decisions regarding the duties of various classes of society and the stages in life (Varṇas and Āśramas); the Lord’s withdrawal from the mortal vision by the power of Yoga.
(Here ends the eleventh Skandha)

devanagari: युगलक्षणवृत्तिश्च कलौ नॄणामुपप्लवः । चतुर्विधश्च प्रलय उत्पत्तिस्त्रिविधा तथा ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: yugalakṣaṇavṛttiśca kalau nṝṇāmupaplavaḥ caturvidhaśca pralaya utpattistrividhā tathā
translation: The characteristics of the four Yugas and the conduct of the public in them; perversity of men in the Kali age, four types of deluges (Pralaya), three types of creations.

devanagari: देहत्यागश्च राजर्षेः विष्णुरातस्य धीमतः । शाखाप्रणयनं ऋषेः मार्कण्डेयस्य सत्कथा । महापुरुषविन्यासः सूर्यस्य जगदात्मनः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: dehatyāgaśca rājarṣeḥ viṣṇurātasya dhīmataḥ śākhāpraṇayanaṃ ṛṣeḥ mārkaṇḍeyasya satkathā mahāpuruṣavinyāsaḥ sūryasya jagadātmanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ sūr class 10 verb]
[genitive single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: Casting off his body by the intelligent royal sage Parīkṣit, the classification of Vedas into different branches by the sage Vyāsa, the sacred story of Mārkaṇḍeya. The disposition of the limbs and weapons of Supreme Person and the ruler of the world as the Sun-goḍ.

devanagari: इति चोक्तं द्विजश्रेष्ठा यत्पृष्टोऽहं इहास्मि वः । लीलावतारकर्माणि कीर्तितानीह सर्वशः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti coktaṃ dvijaśreṣṭhā yatpṛṣṭo līlāvatārakarmāṇi kīrtitānīha sarvaśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ kīrt class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kīrt class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ kīrt class 10 verb]

translation: In this way, O great Brāhmaṇas, whatever I have been asked by you about the sportive activities, incarnations and other acts of the Lord have been described to you by me in all details.

devanagari: पतितः स्खलितश्चार्तः क्षुत्त्वा वा विवशो ब्रुवन् । हरये नम इत्युच्चैः मुच्यते सर्वपातकात् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: patitaḥ skhalitaścārtaḥ kṣuttvā vā vivaśo bruvan haraye nama ityuccaiḥ mucyate sarvapātakāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ skhal class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ skhal ]

translation: A person who involuntarily and unintentionally utters loudly “Salutations to Hari” while falling (in a well, etc.), stumbling (on a staircase, etc.), being afflicted or after sneezing, become rid of all sins.

devanagari: सङ्कीर्त्यमानो भगवान् अनन्तः । श्रुतानुभावो व्यसनं हि पुंसाम् । प्रविश्य चित्तं विधुनोत्यशेषं । यथा तमोऽर्कोऽभ्रमिवातिवातः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅkīrtyamāno bhagavān anantaḥ śrutānubhāvo vyasanaṃ hi puṃsām praviśya cittaṃ vidhunotyaśeṣaṃ yathā tamo
translation: It is the experience of saintly persons that when his name or praise is devoutly chanted or his glory is listened to with devotion, the Infinite, glorious Lord enters their heart and definitely shakes off cleanly and completely all the sins, just as the sun dispels darkness or (if you doubt the sun’s ability to reach inside caves or cavities the Lord does so) like the tempestuous winds scattering the clouds.

devanagari: मृषा गिरस्ता ह्यसतीरसत्कथा । न कथ्यते यद्भगवानधोक्षजः । तदेव सत्यं तदुहैव मङ्गलं । तदेव पुण्यं भगवद्गुणोदयम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛṣā girastā hyasatīrasatkathā na kathyate yadbhagavānadhokṣajaḥ tadeva satyaṃ taduhaiva maṅgalaṃ tadeva puṇyaṃ bhagavadguṇodayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ras class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ras class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ras class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb]

translation: [1] They are indeed futile utterances and unrighteous stories (leading to hell) which do not speak of Lord Viṣṇu (who is above sense-perception). That alone is (how really happy it is to state that) alone is auspicious and meritorious which extol the Lord’s excellent attributes.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Anvitārthaprakāśikā. alternately explains: The stories which do not deal with the theme of the Lord may be true but are fruitless. They may be interesting but they incur sin and are the cause of hell. The stories of good persons (not related with the Lord) are also devoid of merit and the narrator though expressing the truth in sweet words is not a pious story-teller. On the other hand, imaginary stories glorifying the Lord are ‘True’. Though they are ruinous to the house-holder stage, they arc auspicious. Stories of the Lord abducting the wives of others told by low-minded people are also meritorious as they describe the excellence of the Lord.
(bhagavataḥ para-dārāharaṇādikam adhamair apuṇyatvena kathitam api tadeva puṇyaṃ yatas tad bhagavato guṇasyaiva na tu doṣasya udayo yasmāt)

devanagari: तदेव रम्यं रुचिरं नवं नवं । तदेव शश्वन्मनसो महोत्सवम् । तदेव शोकार्णवशोषणं नृणां । यदुत्तमःश्लोकयशोऽनुगीयते ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadeva ramyaṃ ruciraṃ navaṃ navaṃ tadeva śaśvanmanaso mahotsavam tadeva śokārṇavaśoṣaṇaṃ nṛṇāṃ yaduttamaḥślokayaśo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ ram class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ram ]
[nominative single from √ ram class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ram class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ram ], [accusative single from √ ram ]

translation: That alone is beautiful and attractive (even it be like the Lord’s entry in the bear Jāmbavān’s den); that alone is sweet (though unpalatable to devotees such as Rāma’s abandonment of Sītā): that alone is ever fresh giving newer delights every time (although the incident or the theme is age- old); that alone is the eternal festivity and source of joy to the mind (even if it be painful like the abduction of Sītā by Rāvaṇa); that alone is the means of drying up the ocean of grief of human beings even though it creates non-attachment towards close relatives like husband or son).

devanagari: न यद्वचश्चित्रपदं हरेर्यशो । जगत्पवित्रं प्रगृणीत कर्हिचित् । तद् ध्वाङ्क्षतीर्थं न तु हंससेवितं । यत्राच्युतस्तत्र हि साधवोऽमलाः ॥ ५०...
english_devnagari: na yadvacaścitrapadaṃ hareryaśo jagatpavitraṃ pragṛṇīta karhicit tad dhvāṅkṣatīrthaṃ na tu haṃsasevitaṃ yatrācyutastatra hi sādhavo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sev class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sev class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ sīv ], [accusative single from √ sīv ]

translation: [2] The speech composed of words with rhetorical beauty but which seldom describes the world purifying glory of Hari, is regarded like a pleasure worthy of enjoyment for worldly persons, where pure-minded ascetics who dwell in (are merged in the Supreme Spirit (Brahman) never like to stay, like swans from the Mānasa lake, who have beautiful dwelling places, do not find pleasure in pools that are the pleasure resorts of crows alone. Where there is Lord Hari, there are the pure sinless saints.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Verses 50, 51, and 52 are practically the same as supra 1.5.10,11,12. Thus repetition is made at the beginning and the end of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa to establish emphatically the greatness of Bhakti (devotion), just as the bījas of a great mantra are uttered at its beginning and end—Anvitārthaprakāśikā.: Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa

devanagari: तद्वाग्विसर्गो जनताघसंप्लवो । यस्मिन् प्रतिश्लोकमबद्धवत्यपि । नामान्यनन्तस्य यशोऽङ्कितानि यत् । श्रृण्वन्ति गायन्ति गृणन्ति साधवः ॥ ५१...
english_devnagari: tadvāgvisargo janatāghasaṃplavo yasmin pratiślokamabaddhavatyapi nāmānyanantasya yaśo śrṛṇvanti gāyanti gṛṇanti sādhavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: That out-flow of words which though not properly composed in every verse, makes good people (saints) hear, sing, and praise the names (which are as if) imprinted with the glory of the Infinite Lord, destroys the sins of the people.

devanagari: नैष्कर्म्यमप्यच्युत भाववर्जितं । न शोभते ज्ञानमलं निरञ्जनम् । कुतः पुनः शश्वदभद्रमीश्वरे । न ह्यर्पितं कर्म यदप्यनुत्तमम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: naiṣkarmyamapyacyuta bhāvavarjitaṃ na śobhate jñānamalaṃ nirañjanam kutaḥ punaḥ śaśvadabhadramīśvare na hyarpitaṃ karma yadapyanuttamam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛ ], [accusative single from √ ṛ ]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Knowledge, (though) actionless and pure, (if) devoid of devotion to the imperishable Lord (Viṣṇu) does not appear sufficiently bright (i.e. does not result in proper, “direct” knowledge. And how can again the action which is always unblissful, even though (performed) without any desire of its fruit, appear bright (i.e. will have purificatory effect if it is not dedicated to God).

devanagari: यशःश्रियामेव परिश्रमः परो । वर्णाश्रमाचारतपःश्रुतादिषु । अविस्मृतिः श्रीधरपादपद्मयोः । गुणानुवादश्रवणादरादिभिर्हरेः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśaḥśriyāmeva pariśramaḥ paro varṇāśramācāratapaḥśrutādiṣu avismṛtiḥ śrīdharapādapadmayoḥ guṇānuvādaśravaṇādarādibhirhareḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The exertions undergone for carrying out the prescribed religious duties pertaining to one’s Varṇa (class in society) and Āśrama (stage in life), penance, Vedic studies, etc. are for the sake of reputation or affluence. But by singing of and listening to the excellences of the lotus-feet of Śrīdhara (Lord Viṣṇu) unfailing remembrance thereof is ensured.

devanagari: अविस्मृतिः कृष्णपदारविन्दयोः । क्षिणोत्यभद्राणि च शं तनोति च । सत्त्वस्य शुद्धिं परमात्मभक्तिं । ज्ञानं च विज्ञानविरागयुक्तम् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: avismṛtiḥ kṛṣṇapadāravindayoḥ kṣiṇotyabhadrāṇi ca śaṃ tanoti ca sattvasya śuddhiṃ paramātmabhaktiṃ jñānaṃ ca vijñānavirāgayuktam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: Non-forgetfulness (i.e. constant memory) of the lotusfeet of Lord Kṛṣṇa annihilates all evils and inauspiciousness; it deepens the tranquillity of the mind and enhances mental purity and devotion to the Supreme Soul; it promotes spiritual knowledge along with self-knowledge and dispassion.

devanagari: यूयं द्विजाग्र्या बत भूरिभागा । यच्छश्वदात्मन्यखिलात्मभूतम् । नारायणं देवमदेवमीशं । अजस्रभावा भजताविवेश्य ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: yūyaṃ dvijāgryā bata bhūribhāgā yacchaśvadātmanyakhilātmabhūtam nārāyaṇaṃ devamadevamīśaṃ ajasrabhāvā bhajatāviveśya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

[nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

translation: Highly fortunate you are, O prominent Brāhmaṇas, in that you have enthroned in your heart the supreme Nārāyaṇa to whom no other god is superior and who is the Inner Controller of all beings and worship him with constant devotion.

devanagari: अहं च संस्मारित आत्मतत्त्वं । श्रुतं पुरा मे परमर्षिवक्त्रात् । प्रायोपवेशे नृपतेः परीक्षितः । सदस्यृषीणां महतां च श्रृण्वताम् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ ca saṃsmārita ātmatattvaṃ śrutaṃ purā me paramarṣivaktrāt prāyopaveśe nṛpateḥ parīkṣitaḥ sadasyṛṣīṇāṃ mahatāṃ ca śrṛṇvatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation: I have also been reminded of the lucid enlightenment of (the knowledge about) the nature of the soul in the (Bhāgavata Purāṇa) which I heard from the lips of the great sage Śrī Śuka in the assembly of those great sages who assembled to listen to it, when king Parīkṣit determined to observe his vow to fast unto death.

devanagari: एतद्वः कथितं विप्राः कथनीयोरुकर्मणः । माहात्म्यं वासुदेवस्य सर्वाशुभविनाशनम् ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: etadvaḥ kathitaṃ viprāḥ kathanīyorukarmaṇaḥ māhātmyaṃ vāsudevasya sarvāśubhavināśanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kath class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ kath class 10 verb]

translation: O Brāhmaṇas! In this way has been narrated to you the great glory of Lord Vāsudeva whose exploits are worthy of being recounted, as it exterminates all evils and inauspiciousness.

devanagari: य एतत्श्रावयेन्नित्यं यामक्षणमनन्यधीः । श्रद्धावान् योऽनुश्रृणुयात् पुनात्यात्मानमेव सः ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etatśrāvayennityaṃ yāmakṣaṇamananyadhīḥ śraddhāvān yo
translation: He who with concentrated attention, and faith and devotion recounts this (Bhāgavata Purāṇa) continuously (every day) or for a period of three hours or for a minute and he who listens to it with faith and devotion purifies his inner soul (and not the physical body as in a bath).

devanagari: द्वादश्यामेकादश्यां वा श्रृण्वन्नायुष्यवान् भवेत् । पठत्यनश्नन् प्रयतः ततो भवत्यपातकी ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: dvādaśyāmekādaśyāṃ vā śrṛṇvannāyuṣyavān bhavet paṭhatyanaśnan prayataḥ tato bhavatyapātakī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ paṭh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ paṭh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ paṭh class 1 verb], [locative single from √ paṭh class 1 verb]
[accusative plural from √ pre class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: He who hears it on the twelfth or eleventh day of every fortnight of the lunar month, will be blest with longevity; while he who reads it with devotion observing a fast (all the while) absolves himself of all sins.

devanagari: पुष्करे मथुरायां च द्वारवत्यां यतात्मवान् । उपोष्य संहितामेतां पठित्वा मुच्यते भयात् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: puṣkare mathurāyāṃ ca dvāravatyāṃ yatātmavān upoṣya saṃhitāmetāṃ paṭhitvā mucyate bhayāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 1 verb]

translation: Observing fast and exercising all the while perfect self-control, a person who reads this compilation (of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa) at holy places like Puṣkara, Mathurā and Dvārakā, is freed from the fear of Saṃsāra.

devanagari: देवता मुनयः सिद्धाः पितरो मनवो नृपाः । यच्छन्ति कामान् गृणतः श्रृण्वतो यस्य कीर्तनात् ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: devatā munayaḥ siddhāḥ pitaro manavo nṛpāḥ yacchanti kāmān gṛṇataḥ śrṛṇvato yasya kīrtanāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yam class 1 verb]

translation: The gods, sages, siddhas, manes (ancestors), Manus and Kings bestow all the desired boons and the objects on a person who chants it, listens to the chanting or glorifies it.

devanagari: ऋचो यजूंषि सामानि द्विजोऽधीत्यानुविन्दते । मधुकुल्या घृतकुल्याः पयःकुल्याश्च तत्फलम् ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛco yajūṃṣi sāmāni dvijo madhukulyā ghṛtakulyāḥ payaḥkulyāśca tatphalam
translation: A Brāhmaṇa who reads this Purāṇa gains the same blessings technically (by the ancients as) “streams of ghee, honey and milk”, as one who gets after studying the Vedas ( Ṛg- veda, Sāmaveda and Yajurveda).

devanagari: पुराणसंहितां एतां अधीत्य प्रयतो द्विजः । प्रोक्तं भगवता यत्तु तत्पदं परमं व्रजेत् ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: purāṇasaṃhitāṃ etāṃ adhītya prayato dvijaḥ proktaṃ bhagavatā yattu tatpadaṃ paramaṃ vrajet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ pre class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O Brāhmaṇas! A person who, with a controlled and pure mind, studies this compilation called the Bhāgavata Purāṇa, attains to that supreme abode (Mokṣa or Vaikuṇṭha) which the Lord described (in this Purāṇa) as a fruit of it.

devanagari: विप्रोऽधीत्याप्नुयात् प्रज्ञां राजन्योदधिमेखलाम् । वैश्यो निधिपतित्वं च शूद्रः शुध्येत पातकात् ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: vipro vaiśyo nidhipatitvaṃ ca śūdraḥ śudhyeta pātakāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative passive third single]

translation: On studying this compilation of the Bhāgavata, a Brāhmaṇa gets insight into spiritual knowledge (known as ṛtambharā prajñā) a Kṣattriya attains sovereignty over the whole earth engirdled by the seas; a Vaiśya becomes the Lord of wealth (like Kubera) while a Śūdra is absolved of sins.

devanagari: कलिमलसंहतिकालनोऽखिलेशो । हरिरितरत्र न गीयते ह्यभीक्ष्णम् । इह तु पुनर्भगवानशेषमूर्तिः । परिपठितोऽनुपदं कथाप्रसङ्गैः ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: kalimalasaṃhatikālano hariritaratra na gīyate hyabhīkṣṇam iha tu punarbhagavānaśeṣamūrtiḥ paripaṭhito
translation: Lord Viṣṇu, the Supreme Ruler of all, who is the destroyer of the mass of sins and impurities of the Kali age, is not so often extolled and sung in other Purāṇas. But on the contrary, in this Purāṇa, the glorious God who abides in all (the universe) has been glorified at every step, as the occasion of the stories (about him) demanded it (or is sung in every account of this work.)

devanagari: तमहमजमनन्तमात्मतत्त्वं । जगदुदयस्थितिसंयमात्मशक्तिम् । द्युपतिभिरजशक्रशङ्कराद्यैः । दुरवसितस्तवमच्युतं नतोऽस्मि ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: tamahamajamanantamātmatattvaṃ jagadudayasthitisaṃyamātmaśaktim dyupatibhirajaśakraśaṅkarādyaiḥ duravasitastavamacyutaṃ nato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jam class 1 verb]

translation: I bow to Lord Acyuta who is unborn and endless or infinite, the principle of consciousness (or the Supreme Self), the embodiment of the power that is capable of creating, sustaining and dissolving the universe, and who is so great that rulers of heaven like gods Brahmā, Indra, Rudra, and others cannot eulogise him with proper comprehension.

devanagari: उपचितनवशक्तिभिः स्व आत्मनि । उपरचितस्थिरजङ्गमालयाय । भगवत उपलब्धिमात्रधाम्ने । सुरऋषभाय नमः सनातनाय ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: upacitanavaśaktibhiḥ sva ātmani uparacitasthirajaṅgamālayāya bhagavata upalabdhimātradhāmne suraṛṣabhāya namaḥ sanātanāya
translation: Salutations to that eternal Lord, the God of gods whose essential nature is absolute, pure consciousness, who, through the instrumentalities of his nine potencies (viz. Prakṛti, Puruṣa, Mahat, Ahaṅkāra and five tan-mātrās) has evolved in himself the abode for all mobile and immobile beings.

devanagari: स्वसुखनिभृतचेतास्तद्व्युदस्तान्यभावोऽपि । अजितरुचिरलीलाकृष्टसारस्तदीयम् । व्यतनुत कृपया यः तत्त्वदीपं पुराणं । तमखिलवृजिनघ्नं व्याससून...
english_devnagari: svasukhanibhṛtacetāstadvyudastānyabhāvo ajitaruciralīlākṛṣṭasārastadīyam vyatanuta kṛpayā yaḥ tattvadīpaṃ purāṇaṃ tamakhilavṛjinaghnaṃ vyāsasūnuṃ nato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation:
I humbly bow down to Śukācārya, the son of Vyāsa, who destroys all sins Śuka—whose mind was firmly established in the Supreme bliss of ātman, and has hence given up the notion of difference and diversity, but whose heart was fascinated and attracted by the stories of the sportive acts of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the unconquerable, and who out of grace extended this Purāṇa which is a light unto the Truth about him.
[back to top]
